title: 楚子 (Viscount of Chu)
reign: 周成王时期 (King Cheng of Zhou era)
notes: Enfeoffed by King Cheng of Zhou with land of 100 li (子男之田), surname Mi. Served King Cheng alongside Bo Qin of Lu, Mou of Wei, Xie of Jin, and Lü Ji of Qi.

key_events: Gained support of people between Yangtze and Han rivers
Attacked Yong (庸), Yang-Yue (杨粤), reached E (鄂)
Declared: 'I am a barbarian, do not follow Chinese titles'
Made son Kang as King of Goudan (句亶王), son Hong as King of E (鄂王), son Zhici as King of Yuezhang (越章王)
Later removed royal titles during King Li of Zhou's reign out of fear

succession: Brother of Xiong Zhihong, killed him to take throne

key_events: Year 6: Zhou people rebelled, King Li fled to Zhi (彘)

notes: After death, three brothers fought for throne. Zhong Xue died, Shu Kan fled to Pu, Ji Xun won.

key_events: Year 16: Zheng Huan Gong first enfeoffed at Zheng

ruler: 熊仪 (Xiong Yi) / 若敖 (Ruo Ao)
key_events: Year 20: King You of Zhou killed by Quanrong, Zhou moved east, Duke Xiang of Qin first made feudal lord
Year 27: Died

ruler: 熊坎 (Xiong Kan) / 霄敖 (Xiao Ao)
熊眴 (Xiong Xun) / 蚡冒 (Fen Mao)
key_events: Year 13: Jin began to have turmoil due to Quwo

ruler: 熊通 (Xiong Tong) / 楚武王 (King Wu)
key_events: Year 17: Quwo Zhuang Bo of Jin killed Lord Xiao Hou of Jin
Year 19: Brother Duan of Zheng rebelled
Year 35: Attacked Sui (随), demanded royal title from Zhou
Year 37: Self-declared King Wu when Zhou refused
Year 51: Died during campaign against Sui
notes: First Chu ruler to declare himself King

ruler: 熊赀 (Xiong Zi) / 楚文王 (King Wen)
key_events: Year 2: Attacked Shen via Deng
Year 6: Attacked Cai, captured Duke Ai of Cai
Year 11: Duke Huan of Qi began hegemony, Chu also began to grow powerful
Year 12: Destroyed Deng

ruler: 熊畑 (Xiong Jiong) / 庄敖 (Zhuang Ao)
notes: Killed by brother Xiong Yun with help from Sui

ruler: 熊恽 (Xiong Yun) / 楚成王 (King Cheng)
key_events: Year 1: Sent tribute to Zhou Emperor, received sacrificial meat, told to control southern barbarians
Year 16: Duke Huan of Qi invaded Chu, made alliance at Jing Shan
Year 33: Captured and humiliated Duke Xiang of Song at Meng
Year 34: Defeated Song at Hong, wounded Duke Xiang who later died
Year 35: Treated Chong'er (later Duke Wen of Jin) with honors
Year 39: Jin defeated Chu at Chengpu
Year 46: Killed by son Shangchen in coup
Year 3: Destroyed Jiang (江)
Year 4: Destroyed Liu (六) and Liao (蓼)
Year 8: Attacked Chen
First 3 years: No decrees, indulged in pleasure
After Wu Ju's remonstration: 'Three years without flying, once flying will reach the sky; three years without calling, once calling will startle the world'
Killed hundreds, promoted hundreds, appointed Wu Ju and Su Cong
Year: Destroyed Yong (庸)
Year 6: Attacked Song, captured 500 chariots
Year 8: Campaign against Luhun Rong, reached Luo, asked about the Nine Tripods' weight at Zhou outskirts
Year 9: Destroyed Ruo'ao clan
Year 13: Destroyed Shu (舒)
Year 16: Attacked Chen, killed Xia Zhengshu, restored Chen
Year 17: Besieged Zheng for 3 months, then spared it; defeated Jin at the river
Year 20: Besieged Song for 5 months until 'exchanging children to eat, splitting bones for fuel'
Year 16: Battle of Yanling against Jin, Jin defeated Chu, wounded King's eye
General Zifan drunk, King killed him
Year 3: Met feudal lords at Shen
Year 7: Built Zhanghua Terrace
Year 8: Destroyed Chen
Year 10: Killed Duke of Cai
Year 12: Overthrown, wandered in wilderness, starved to death at Shen Hai's home

notes: Killed himself after believing King Ling had returned

ruler: 弃疾 (Qiji) / 熊居 (Xiong Ju) / 楚平王 (King Ping)
key_events: Restored Chen and Cai
Year 2: Sent Fei Wuji to Qin for bride for Crown Prince Jian, took her himself instead
Year 6: Crown Prince Jian fled to Song after Fei Wuji's slander
Killed Wu She and Wu Shang
Wu Xu (伍子胥) fled to Wu
Year 1: Fei Wuji executed
Year 4: Three Wu princes fled to Chu
Year 7: Chu attacked Wu, defeated at Yuzhang
Year 10: Wu (Helü, Wu Zixu, Bo Pi) invaded with Tang and Cai, captured Ying, humiliated King Ping's tomb
King fled to Yunmeng, then to Sui
Qin sent 500 chariots to help
Year 11: Defeated Wu at Ji
Year 12: Moved capital to Ruo (鄀)
Year 20: Destroyed Dun (顿) and Hu (胡)
Year 27: Died in army while saving Chen from Wu
Year 2: Bai Gong (Sheng) recalled from Wu
Year 8: Bai Gong killed Lingyin Zi Xi, held King Hui captive; Ye Gong saved the king
Year: Destroyed Chen
Year 13: Wu attacked
Year 16: Yue destroyed Wu
Year 42: Destroyed Cai
Year 44: Destroyed Qi (杞)
Year 1: Destroyed Ju (莒) in the north
Year 8: Wei Wen Hou, Han Wu Zi, Zhao Huan Zi first made feudal lords

ruler: 熊疑 (Xiong Yi) / 楚悼王 (King Dao)
key_events: Year 2: Three Jin attacked Chu to Chengqiu
Year 4: Attacked Zhou
Year 9: Attacked Han, took Fushu
Year 11: Three Jin defeated Chu at Daliang and Yuguan
Year 4: Shu attacked Chu, took Zifang
Year 10: Wei took Luyang

ruler: 熊良夫 (Xiong Liangfu) / 楚宣王 (King Xuan)
key_events: Year 6: Zhou Emperor congratulated Qin Xian Gong
Year 30: Qin enfeoffed Wei Yang at Shang, invaded Chu southward

ruler: 熊商 (Xiong Shang) / 楚威王 (King Wei)
key_events: Year 6: Zhou Xian Wang sent sacrificial meat to Qin Hui Wang
Year 7: Defeated Qi at Xuzhou

ruler: 熊槐 (Xiong Huai) / 楚怀王 (King Huai)
key_events: Year 1: Zhang Yi became Qin chancellor
Year 6: Chu general Zhao Yang defeated Wei at Xiangling
Year 11: Alliance of six states against Qin, King Huai was leader
Year 16: Zhang Yi deceived King Huai with promise of 600 li of land for breaking with Qi
Year 17: Battle of Danyang - Qin killed 80,000 Chu soldiers, took Hanzhong
Year 18: Zhang Yi captured then released
Year 30: Lured to Wu Pass by Qin, captured, died in Qin Year 3 of Qingxiang Wang
Year 1: Qin attacked, killed 50,000, took 15 cities
Year 3: King Huai died in Qin
Year 7: Made peace with Qin
Year 14: Met Qin King at Wan
Year 15: Attacked Qi with allies, took Huaibei
Year 19: Ceded Shangyong and Hannorth to Qin
Year 21: Qin general Bai Qi took Ying, burned royal tombs at Yiling
Year 22: Qin took Wu and Qianzhong commanderies
Year 23: Recovered 15 cities from Qin
Year 27: Sent 30,000 troops to help attack Yan

ruler: 熊元 (Xiong Yuan) / 楚考烈王 (King Kaolie)
key_events: Year 1: Ceded Zhou to Qin for peace
Year 6: Sent Jing Yang to save Zhao from Qin
Year 22: Moved capital to Shouchun, named it Ying
notes: Appointed Left Minister as Lingyin, enfeoffed at Wu, titled Lord Chunshen

key_events: Year 3: Qin and Wei attacked Chu
Year 9: Qin destroyed Han
Year 1: Yan crown prince Dan sent Jing Ke to assassinate Qin King
Year 2: Qin attacked, took over 10 cities
Year 3: Qin destroyed Wei
Year 4: Qin general Wang Jian defeated Chu at Qi, killed general Xiang Yan
Year 5: Wang Jian and Meng Wu destroyed Chu, captured King Fuchu
notes: Last ruler of Chu, state annexed as Qin commandery
